info.title: Pantheon API
info.description:
Official web API for the Pantheon community server network.

For more information please see the [Github repository](https://github.com/Pantheon-Community/PantheonAPI).

get.description: IP address information endpoint, useful for debugging proxies.

200.description: IP address of request, may be `::` if viewing from the same network.
OpenAPI specification for the FoundationX API.

post.description: Endpoint for completing the Discord OAuth2 login flow.

200.description: User data and a token for making elevated requests to the API.

post.description: Endpoint for ending an existing OAuth2 session.
Endpoint for refreshing an existing OAuth2 session.

200.description: User data and a token for making elevated requests to the API.

get.description: Endpoint for looking up Steam user info from Discord IDs.

200.description:
Array of the relevant Steam users for each of the the given Steam IDs.

Note that this does map 1:1 with the given Discord ID array, since a Discord ID may not be associated with any steam users.

get.description: Endpoint for looking up Discord user info from Steam IDs.

200.description:
Array of the relevant Discord user(s) for the given Steam IDs.

Note that this does map 1:1 with the given Steam ID array, since a Steam ID can be associated with any number of users.

get.description: Endpoint for getting basic information about a Steam user.

Endpoint for getting information about the currently logged-in user.

Note that the `/login` endpoint returns a superset of this information, so you do not need to call this after logging in.
Endpoint for getting all the current sessions of the currently logged-in user.
Endpoint for getting specifically the Discord Steam connections for the currently logged-in user.

delete.description: Endpoint for clearing the primary Steam connection of the logged-in user's account.

put.description: Endpoint for setting the primary Steam connection of the logged-in user's account.

BadRequestError400.description:
Error thrown when an invalid request is made, such as missing required body fields or passing in a string where a number was expected.

Note this does not include errors around the validity of the authorization header.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Status/400)

example.title: Invalid Origin
example.description: The origin header of your request ("https://example.com") isn't in the approved client URLs list.

ForbiddenError403.description:
Error thrown when a request is made with insufficient permissions, or to do something that is not allowed no matter the permissions.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Status/403)

example.title: Missing Permissions
example.description: You do not have the required permissions to do this action.

NotFoundError404.description:
Error thrown when a resource does not exist in the database.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Status/404)

SecondaryRequestError501.description:
Error thrown when an API call made by the server to another server fails.

Although this **should** have a status code of 502 (Bad Gateway), Cloudflare likes to overwrite 502 errors with its own error page, so 501 is used instead.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Status/502)

example.title: Token Request Failure
example.description: Failed to obtain a Discord access token, the supplied code or redirect URI may be invalid.

UnauthorizedError401.description:
Error thrown when a request is made with missing or malformed credentials, such as having an invalid site token.

Has status code 401 (Unauthorized), since the user has failed authentication.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Status/401)

example.title: Missing Token
example.description: A token was not provided in the "Authorization" header.

RateLimited429.description:
Too many requests, rate limited.

[IETF Draft 8](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-httpapi-ratelimit-headers-08)

RateLimit.description:
- 'r' (remaining) = number of requests you can currently make before getting rate limited.
- 't' = number of seconds until the next rate limit reset.

RateLimit-Policy.description:
- 'q' (quota) = number of requests that can ever be made before getting rate limited.
- 'w' = number of seconds between rate limit resets.

IsoString.description:
A string in ISO format, used to store dates.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601

UserToken.description:
An authorization token used by our users.

Also serves as a Discord OAuth2 access token.

https://docs.discord.com/developers/topics/oauth2

PluginToken.description: An authorization token used by our SCP:SL plugins.

Ip.description: An IP address, not uniformly structured!

UserAgent.example: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:148.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/148.0

Origin.description: An origin URL of a client.

UserSessionId.description:
Way of uniquely referring to a user session.

Although the **accessToken** field of a user session is also unique, using it to externally identify user sessions has obvious security drawbacks.

title.description: The error described in a few words.

description.description: A sentence that explains how the error occurred.
description.example: A user with this ID does not exist in the database.

SteamUser.description:
Steam user information, sourced from either in-game player info or Discord user connections.

Note that tracking fields (**firstSeenAt**, **lastSeenAt**, and **timesSeen**) are only updated from in-game player info - never from Discord user conections. This is because in-game activity is much more useful to us than website activity.

username.description:
Latest username associated with this Steam ID.

This may come from in-game player info or a Discord user connection.

firstSeenAt.description: When this Steam ID was first seen in-game.

lastSeenAt.description: When this Steam ID was last seen in-game.

timesSeen.description: Number of times this Steam ID has been seen in-game.

actionCount.description: This does not include the creation or refreshing of the session.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Logging in, logging out, and refreshing.

tags.name: Lookups
tags.description: Endpoints used by our other services for Steam and Discord user lookups.
